title: Level 139
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 139: " The Aquatic Express"}} {{Underwater Theme}} {{Class 1e}} == Description == {{Blurquote|image=Asphyxia4.jpeg|person=███████ that was on level 139. the first to enter it after the flicker incident.|quote-text= One second, I drowned in level 7... The next I'm on a train, that's under the water. I remember being so confused, it was unlike any level I'd seen so far.}} '''Level 139''' Also known as "The Aquatic Expressway." That takes the appearance of a large finite modern train that stretches on for miles on end. It's reported that there could be more trains, and there's strong evidence, but it's not confirmed. Each seat is facing forwards, having a small table for any wanderers have belongings, in rare cases the car you're in may not have a table at all. Outside, the windows can depict a light blue ocean that seemingly is a void downwards, having no signs of fish, coral, or even sand. However, during the trains journey, sometimes it can depict a black endless void, mimicking our ocean with its different zones. Each car is around 50 degrees in temperature, or 10 degrees in celsius. The train car goes around 30 miles per hour, but it is unnoticeable throughout the journey. Wanderers report being seasick when first entering the level, but overtime they eventually become comfortable in the car they are in. Also, the Train's always stay underwater, they never go above surface level, always either staying in the dark zone or the light zone. Another thing is that train seemingly floats, it never sinks despite its sheer size. It's also worth noting that there are no tracks. Despite this, the train still acts like it is, with its movement, even though it's under the water. {{Blurimage|caption= A popular photo of level 139.|align=left|file=119.png}} Transferring to each car is impossible due to the amount of pressure in the ocean. Opening one of the doors leading to another cabin (or window.) will crush the entire cabin car, killing the wanderer in the process. This is due to the amount of pressure that's outside the ocean compared to the inside. Each car is connected, but as stated before, it's in the open, meaning opening the door will crush the wanderer. It's also worth noting that wanderers that are together, will be separated into different train cars. But can still see each other through a door's windows. Wanderers that enter the level together claim that they were only one car from each other. Searching inside one of the cabinets above the seats are almond water and other food one may need to survive. However, there is a limited supply of the resources. Any belongings the wanderer has will be in one of the cabinets above the seats. Usually, their belongings are placed at the cabinet closest to them. == Entities == No entities have been documented that reside on this level. == Events == === Steep Incline/Decline === Sometimes the train experiences inclines and declines, it's hard to notice due to how short or long the incline is. However, it'll still have rough inclines or declines. During the journey one may feel the train go faster for a moment before it starts to speed up. The wanderer will be flown backwards on their seat, or if they're standing up, they get flown to the back of train car. The train goes around 80mph during this state compared to its usual speed. Eventually it'll slow down and go back to the normal. Same things go for declines but instead, the wanderer is flown forward. These declines and inclines are how they transfer from one zone to another. For example, if the train is in the light zone but needs to go to the dark zone, it'll do a steep decline. == The Flicker == Like most levels, '''Level 139''' was affected by the flicker. If you were in the level during the flicker, the train lights would lose power, making the train completely dark besides being illuminated by the outside waters. Eventually the train would go into the dark zone. After that wanderers say they blacked out and woke up in another level, not recalling what exactly made them blackout. It is reported that each level the wanderer was in was safe, or the ones that were interviewed said they were. After this the level was unenterable, the usual entrance methods weren't working. However, after 2 years have passed since the flicker incident. After someone drowned in Level 7, they were back in '''Level 139'''. After that happened, more entrances of the level started to appear. Although it's unknown how it fixed itself or appeared again, which leads many theories like the one mentioned earlier of multiple trains. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Drowning in any level has a slight chance of it leading here. In [[level 7]], finding a Train under the water and entering it will lead here. Finding a door that says "Aquatic Express Station" will lead here. === Stations === The only way to exit the level is to get off at one of the stations. That being said, Noclipping won't get you out of this level. There can also be multiple kinds of one station, however these are the only 4 recorded. The stations are also enclosed, and completely sealed, so the pressure from the doors opening wouldn't crush the whole train car. About 8 cars of the train can get off, so groups of wanderers are reunited at the station. {{Blurimage|caption= One of the stations, it leads to level 11|file=119station.png|align=right}} ==== Station 11 ==== Station 11 is the station that leads to [[Level 11]] of the backrooms. Going up the escalators will lead to Level 11, however, going to a gray door behind the escalators will lead to a flooded hallway and eventually will lead to [[level 11.11]] instead. The station has a modern design with seats, with trashcans that are overflowing with trash. The Station is around 50 degrees in Fahrenheit, or 10 degrees Celsius. ==== Station 2 ==== This station leads to [[Level 2]] of the backrooms. It is a dark gray station with a hallway that leads the level. Puddles are everywhere with gray, copper, bronze, and black pipes lining the walls. The stations seats are damp and everywhere is littered with trash. The station is also very poorly built, with cracks in the walls, and pipes leaking. The area is 40 degrees Fahrenheit or 4.44 degrees Celsius. ==== Station 0 ==== This station leads to [[Level 0]] of the backrooms. This station is padded with yellows walls and a yellow tiled floor. The station is defined by its loud buzzing sounds from the lights. The seats are generic with trash cans lined up on each side. Going down a bright yellow hallway leads to the level. The area is also around 50 degrees in temperature, or 10 degrees Celsius. If the area is hotter or colder, than it may lead to a sublevel like level [[Level 0.66|0.66]]. ==== Station 5 ==== This station will lead to [[Level 5]]. It is a 1950's vintage looking station, with red carpet and white walls. The seats are red with gold rose patterns while vintage music plays in this station. With old Lamp designs with wooden trashcans. A white door that says "EMPLOYEES ONLY" leads to a level 5's boiler room. However, going up a flight of steps will lead to the hotel part of the level. This Station is around 41 degrees Fahrenheit, or 5 degrees Celsius, getting hotter if you're near the boiler room door.
